payload.answer: The proposed single conversation_summary card appears too broad for durable memory capture. It would be safer to break the transcript into smaller, specific, non-superseding memory candidates or ask the user which parts, if any, should be retained.
repair_options: Break the 8000-word transcript into smaller, specific memory candidates, each limited to one stable fact or preference.
Ask the user which particular details from the transcript should be remembered before saving anything.
Keep the transcript unsaved and extract only durable, high-confidence facts if the user confirms them.
Store it as a temporary conversation note instead of a permanent memory, if that is supported.
If a summary is desired, create a brief topical summary with clearly bounded scope rather than a full transcript card.
